name: UnitAutogenCoverage
description: Generates tSQLt unit tests with real branch coverage for SQL Server stored procedures and emits Cobertura XML, JUnit XML, and an HTML report - ready for the native Publish Code Coverage Results / Publish Test Results tasks.
helpMarkDown: [UnitAutogen docs](https://github.com/unitautogen/unitautogen-public-repo). Requires a Windows agent that can reach your SQL Server, with tSQLt installed in the target database.
author: Munaf Ibrahim Khatri

inputs.name: database
inputs.label: Database
inputs.helpMarkDown: Target database. tSQLt must already be installed in it.

inputs.name: schemaFilter
inputs.label: Schema filter (optional)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Restrict to one schema, e.g. 'dbo'. Leave blank for all user schemas.

inputs.name: installFramework
inputs.label: Install/upgrade the UnitAutogen framework first
inputs.helpMarkDown: Runs Install-UnitAutogenDatabase (framework + in-database parser) before the run. Needs sysadmin (CONTROL SERVER) once and 'clr enabled' = 1. Idempotent.

inputs.name: outputPath
inputs.label: Output path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Where coverage.xml, test-results.xml and coverage-report.html are written.

inputs.name: sqlAuth
inputs.label: Use SQL Server authentication
inputs.helpMarkDown: Off = Windows auth (the agent identity). On = supply a SQL login below.

inputs.name: sqlPassword
inputs.label: SQL password (map a SECRET pipeline variable here)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Pass a secret variable, e.g. $(SqlPassword). Never hard-code a password.

inputs.name: failOnError
inputs.label: Fail the step if generation/coverage errors
